reviews.ownerResponse:
We’re sorry to hear that this was your experience. At Steve Smith Trial Lawyers, we take concerns about professionalism, communication, and client care very seriously, and it’s never our intention for any client to feel disrespected or unheard.

Because confidentiality is central to the attorney–client relationship, we’re unable to address specific details in a public forum. That said, the feedback you’ve shared does not reflect the standards we hold ourselves to, and we would welcome the opportunity to better understand what occurred.

We encourage you to contact our firm directly so we can review your concerns and address them appropriately. Client trust and respect are priorities for us, and we are always open to constructive feedback that helps us improve.

reviews.text: Steve Smith and his team will hit the ground running and provide excellent legal services in a no nonsense and professional way.

Steve Smith and his team are not only extremely knowledgeable, but supportive and sensitive to the fact that a child custody case can be emotionally distressing. Steve and his team are highly responsive, check in frequently, make sure I understand what is happening at all times, and explain everything to me in terms I can easily understand.
If you're seeking legal help as a child custody client, call Steve Smith’s legal team today.

My ex husband used this lawyer after I left an abusive four year relationship to take my son from me. My ex husband told me he wanted primary custody- and that his lawyer “Stephen Smith” wanted me to sign my parental rights responsibilities away but he “wouldn’t let his lawyer do that”  (this is all in text conversation) at this point my son was being withheld from me for over six months, my attorney could not show up for our case, I had just gotten a new place and was spending money I didn’t have so I decided to sign an agreement with no representation at Stephen Smiths office (my ex husbands lawyer) I showed up, signed the paper and left. Only to find out I had indeed signed away my parental responsibilities and rights. I know stephen smith orchestrated this as my ex husband expressed in text that he did not agree with Stephen wanting to do that and would not let him trick me into signing it away. Two years later, my ex husband blocked me a year ago (last October after my son and I discussed trick or treating together)  I have not seen or talked to my son in a year. I do not know what my son thinks. I served my ex husband and did not file for a modification (6months ago)- I still have no lawyer at this point so I was holding off on filing. For having my ex husband served I owe Stephen (2,500$) for filing against me after I had my ex husband served. I hope Stephen uses some of this money to get his mental health in check, if not a face lift will surely help with the sagging and frown lines for his next court appearance. I am spending the next year saving up, I have documentation of everything. Once I can get representation and get in front of a judge this will come to a swift end, I will be able to see my son. I hope this law office will be held responsible for their part (they should be shut down). If any lawyers who read this want to reach out please do, I am starting to share my story and have all documentation.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We are truly sorry to hear how painful and distressing this situation has been for you. Matters involving children and family relationships are deeply emotional, and we recognize how difficult these experiences can be.

That said, our firm strongly disputes the allegations made in this review. Attorneys are ethically and legally prohibited from misleading any party, particularly in family law matters, and we do not “orchestrate” outcomes or advise individuals to waive parental rights without proper legal process and safeguards in place. Any agreements executed in our office are governed by court procedures and judicial approval, not unilateral action by counsel.

Because client confidentiality and court rules prevent us from discussing the details of any case in a public forum, we cannot address specific claims here. However, we take concerns about professionalism and ethics seriously. If you believe there has been an error or misconduct, we encourage you to raise those concerns through the appropriate legal channels so they can be reviewed fully and fairly.

We sincerely hope you are able to obtain the representation and support you need moving forward and wish you the best as you continue to pursue resolution through the court system.

reviews.text: Waste of money! My advice find someone else!
reviews.ownerResponse: We understand how frustrating legal outcomes can be. What someone hopes will happen and what the law allows are often very different, and results are ultimately determined by facts, evidence, and the court—not personal preference. Our role is to advocate aggressively within the bounds of the law and work toward the best possible outcome given those realities. We regret that your experience did not meet your expectations and wish you the best moving forward.

reviews.text:
A good rule of thumb: never trust a law office that claims to specialize in everything—medical malpractice, divorce, wills and estates, criminal law, and more. Real professionals focus and build expertise. When a firm spreads itself thin across so many areas, you’re not getting true specialists but rather generalists who claim to do it all, but rarely do anything well.

Steve Smith Trial Lawyers is a perfect example of this problem. From my experience, there is a constant turnover in both attorneys and support staff, which is a major red flag. A reputable firm retains skilled employees who bring stability and experience to clients’ cases. Instead, this office feels like a revolving door, which makes it nearly impossible to trust that anyone there will follow through or even stay long enough to see a case through properly.

If you happen to be unlucky enough to get assigned a Steve Smith attorney through the criminal justice system, do yourself a favor and request a different lawyer immediately. Having competent, stable representation is critical in those circumstances, and this firm does not provide it.

What you end up with is a ragtag group of unprofessionals, operating more like a patchwork crew than a serious law practice. Professionalism, consistency, and competence should be the bare minimum for a law firm, and unfortunately, Steve Smith Trial Lawyers doesn’t meet that standard.
If you value your time, money, and peace of mind, I strongly suggest looking elsewhere.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We appreciate you taking the time to share your perspective. While we respectfully disagree with the characterizations in this review, we recognize that clients and community members may have differing views on how law firms structure their practices.

Our firm is built around experienced attorneys who focus on specific practice areas within a collaborative team model, allowing clients to benefit from both subject-matter knowledge and collective resources. We are committed to professionalism, ethical representation, and continuity of care in every matter we handle.

Because we cannot discuss individual experiences or assignments publicly, we encourage anyone with concerns to reach out directly or seek independent counsel to ensure they feel confident in their representation.

reviews.text:
I had the great pleasure of working with Richard Lucas and Tyler Soucy. They were both professional, responsive, and, most importantly, very passionate about my case.

An elected official was attempting to interfere with my First Amendment rights, in what was an unprecedented case in this particular state.

Not only did Mr. Lucas and Mr. Soucy understand the facts of my case, but they also grasped the precedent it would set. In addition to fully reviewing the evidence and exhibits I provided, they went above and beyond to uncover other exhibits about the defendant that I did not have in my possession.

On the day of the trial, Mr. Soucy was masterful in his cross-examination; he slowly built our case to expose the defendant's frivolous attempt to use the court to chill free speech. I lost count of how many hypocritical corners he backed the defendant into.

To say that I highly recommend the team at Steve Smith Trial Lawyers would be an enormous understatement. Mr. Lucas and Mr. Soucy are both champions for justice and incredible trial attorneys. I was very fortunate to have worked with these fine gentlemen.

Complete scam artists.
Waisted our money. Google did a better job.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for taking the time to share your experience, and we want to sincerely apologize for the frustration and disappointment this situation has caused — especially during what we know is already a deeply emotional and complex journey. We did encounter unexpected issues with our scheduling platform, and unfortunately, your consultation was one of the appointments affected. This is not the level of service we hold ourselves to, and we take full responsibility. Our team acted quickly to address the issue and ensure all impacted appointments were rescheduled as a top priority. We understand how important every step of the adoption process is, and the last thing we want is to add more stress. Your journey is important, and we’re here to support you however we can. If you'd like to connect directly so we can assist further, please don’t hesitate to reach out to our team.

The Planning Board (PB) in our town became involved with sustaining a voter approved (passed) ordinance describing the requirements for siting a Large Scale Wind Farm (LWES).  The town's ordinance was challenged at the state level. To help the town with this matter, the town retained the services of Upton & Hatfield (Justin Richardson).  Mr. Richardson guided the town with the preparation of the required briefs and then argued the briefs on several occasions in front of the NH state regulatory bodies .  Largely as a consequence of the efforts of Justin Richardson, the town prevailed in this matter.  The state's decision for the town was a very significant change in state policy favoring local rule.

It can be said by those involved that Justin was instrumental to the decision and that without Justin's expertise, experience and legal skills, the towns would have most likely not prevailed.

reviews.text: Our family had a number of individual, but contiguous land parcels that we wanted to consolidate into a single parcel in an attempt to reduce our property tax burden.  We had originally been told by the tax assessor that we would need to go through some expensive exercises to accomplish this.  Mr. Cabot took the time to listen to our issue, and proposed a very cost effective solution of consolidating the individual parcel deeds into one using his firms services.  After receiving a quote for services, we agreed to have Cabot Legal Services perform our deed consolidation.  We have had bad experiences in the past with complicated land sales and right-of-way issues that always ended up costing significantly more than we were told by the attorney's helping us with those issues, so I was skeptical about the quote provided being the actual costs that we would be charged.  I know that a significant amount of work went into our deed consolidation by Cabot Legal Services, but when the legal services were complete we were billed the amount quoted.  In addition, a few months later when we received the property tax bill it was significantly reduced as expected.  We didn't have to contact the town/county to confirm that the deed consolidation was properly filed, because Cabot Legal Services had done everything proper and correct.  Thank you, Cabot Legal Services.

There is far more to the journey of seeking justice than the oftentimes timely and distant resolve of judgment. Know that Sally Morris will provide you with the support and sustenance that justice, the journey through it, and ultimately judgment requires. She is an excellent attorney. Equally and possibly most importantly, she is a caring, empathetic, and understanding human being. There are lots of attorneys who are experienced in legal processes and protocols. Sally Morris is more than an attorney. She is someone who understands the human component of your journey to find justice, she prepares you for it and travels the journey with you. Keep her at the top of your list of attorneys to consider for your employment law and civil litigation needs. Thank you, Sally.
Sally Morris has been my attorney since 8/2023.  She is incredibly knowledgeable, responsive and professional.   I highly recommend her services.  Carrie is her assistant, and she is top notch too!

Not really into reviews. However, after this experience, never feeling like I would receive justice, feeling demoralized, Sally made me feel welcomed to my new future. It's very hard to describe, just saying how I feel. She helped me realize and accept the bad had happened and it was time to stand up for myself. She got this point across with incredible class and empathy. I started not to feel so little. Within weeks she pulled my case together and I started to feel like I was myself again. See, no matter how confident and experienced you are you can always be knocked down, but it matters who you have around you which determines how and even if you will stand back up. Sally didn't pull me along instead she explained things in a way that helped me get the fight back. She helped me feel worth standing up for myself. As you can imagine it is all very jarring if you have to seek out a labor attorney. Something bad must have had to have taken place right? So I say again, it matters who you have with you. Sally was, well, with me and she was with me the whole time. The value I place on her is so incredibly overwhelming larger than transactional. She is my friend for life. She became a mentor for me and to me. Not only did I receive justice but I grew even stronger ready for the next issue to solve. Really appreciate you Sally. Always a phone call away for you and your family!!! (I'm scouring my phone right after I write this for a photo hang tight)

If you want results that matter, hands-down, look no further, Sally Morris is your answer! We were late in securing a Law Firm that could help expedite a real estate closing, on short notice. I emailed Paul Driscoll on Friday, informed by his Assistant Gail immediately that he was away, would be back Monday. I explained my urgency. Paul emailed me Saturday and we spoke Sunday afternoon. By Monday, 10am, Paul said we should be ready to close by Friday. Amazing (!). And we were and did close Friday...Double Amazing. Extremely happy w/ Paul and his Team's ability to Get It Done. I highly recommend NHD and specifically Paul, Gail and Jaimee.
Thank you ALL for making what 4 other Law firms said 'couldn't be done', DONE.
5 Stars, all the way.

Sam Johnson gave me his attention and time to carefully assess legal matters I was faced with and gave me clear and sound advice to help me make important legal decisions. I highly recommend this firm. Thank you for your help!

Aaron K Baltes has represented me in various legal matters from the time I was 18-19 years old. I am 36 now and thanks to Aaron I have a clean record. I had found myself in a little trouble in my younger years, but he was able to get any charges either filed or completely dismissed. He really went to bat for me and worked hard to ensure I had the best possible outcome in all cases. From day one he’s treated me with respect and has always spoken to me with empathy and compassion.
Recently I needed his help as I was going through a divorce. Aaron was always quick to return my calls and emails. At times he listened to me go on and on and always gave me the best advice. There were times he reached out to my ex husband on my behalf so that I didn’t have to myself and was actually able to get my ex to cooperate which he hadn’t done until hearing from Aaron.
Should I ever find myself needing legal representation again in any form, I will call Aaron with the utmost confidence knowing he will get everything worked out.

A terrific law firm. The staff was fantastic and the lawyers are very knowledgeable and do a wonderful job representing you. The firm deals in various types of law and are highly rated. A lot of Maine's top lawyers work there. Definitely a place to go if you need any type of legal advice.

reviews.text:
A short time ago, I was in motor vehicle accident. Being rear-ended while driving home. I was taken to the hospital, received treatment and was bed ridden for months..My vehicle was totalled.
I needed to seek help, due to the insurance companies were only wanting to pay minimal amounts in my opinion. A friend of mine who is an business attorney suggested Rob Stephen,due to his well known experience in personal injury claims.
I called his office and was pleasantly greeted Amy who took down all relevant info. Rob Stephen shortly called me back and said he would immediately start work on my case. Due to the fact that I was bed-ridden, Rob came to my house to gather more info and personally explain all possible avenues with going forward, what to expect, times involved, all medical bills (present and future) and various future outcomes in my case based on his vast experience.
Rob and Amy put my mind to rest, Rob dealt with all billing issues and negotiations with medical debt (insurances). He and Amy actually put my wife's worries to rest also..(happy wife is a happy life).
Soon, as predicted by Rob and Amy, the time frames were all met, and benchmarks on my case whizzed bye.
The whole time, I was kept completely up to date on all correspondence between all parties involved..Rob diplomatically and professionally negotiated through much difficulty an amount that exceeded all of our highest expectations.
Within two weeks I had a fat check that covered the replacement cost of my vehicle, all medical bills (past, present and future) and a compensation for all the months of suffering I went through.
I am so extremely happy and satisfied completely to have hired Rob with my personal injury case. I am proud to say I've never met a more professional legal team as Rob and Amy..I now call them my trusted friends....Thank you Rob and Amy
Larry Ferrante
reviews.ownerResponse: Larry, it has been a pleasure representing you.  Thank you for the confidence you have in your services. Attorney Rob Stephen

reviews.text: Represented my divorce case extremely well.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you, Skip, for your 5-star rating.  I do not have to tell you what is involved in contested divorce (and now post-divorce) litigation.  Our divorce laws give judges such broad discretion that one side can overreach to the detriment (and expense) of both parties.  When coupled with the human imperfection of judges, whose rulings can leave both sides befuddled, the outcomes are even more uncertain.

Thank you, Russ, for your 5-star review.  I regret that I am only now getting set up to respond.  While I would enjoy hearing from you, I hope that it is not because you need my services.  Going to court is not something that anyone wants to do.  That said, as I have said for three decades, "If you're going to court, I can help."  Take care.

As we all know family court is not a quick easy process but from beginning to end Chris made me feel like I  had nothing to worry about and I didn’t! Over a year later not only did Chris help me get what I initially wanted but I got MUCH more than expected ! I promise you whatever your legal issue is if you decide to work with Chris and Dirigo Law group you can rest easy knowing you have the most professional, experienced , knowledgeable and caring firm you will ever work with!
These guys are great!

I hired Ted Dillworth due to a few recommendations. I feel as though he did not help my case, but maybe even made it worse. I supplied a huge stack of supporting information, facts on everything I was fighting against. When important areas come up and I asked "did you mention this which I gave you in the paperwork" his response was "I must have missed that". I got that response when i pointed things out several times. Also when asked to look into a previous record, I was told he would do so on several occasions and he never actually did. If he actually would have done his job and fought for me, the outcome would have been different. This being said, I am extremely unhappy and I feel as though I did not get what I paid for.
Ted is the most professional and friendly lawyer I've ever encountered.

Amber is dedicated and passionate but instead of owning her mistake, she tried to back peddle with us and projected back on us that she “misunderstood” us.   That was hard to understand since it had been discussed a number of times.   Her solution to us being upset?   She dropped us as clients instead of making us whole.  So proceed with caution but education advocate must be treated gingerly or beware of the gauntlet.  Truly expensive and disappointing experience.  Legal representation based on feelings can’t provide successful solutions.  Beware.

reviews.text: I was referred to this lawyer by a lawyer from my hometown in NC. He went thru NOELA and found Pross. Sent me the contact info and said she would be the best to help me in Maine. I called on Presidents’ Day, got no answer and left a voicemail. No return call. I called again today, 4 days later, looking to get an appointment. We need a POA and help with securing my mother’s assets as my dad passed away 30 days ago. I was informed that I would need to do a consultation first and then schedule a second appointment to complete the requests. The first available date would not be until sometime in April. Who knows when the second appointment will be. Very inefficient. I’m sure Ms Pross is a great attorney as I value my attorneys opinions. My advice to anyone with aging parents, call about 5 years before you remotely think you might need this attorney as they are just swamped with clients and can’t get to you for at least 30-45 days. Even when I explained this to the receptionist, she did not seem the least bit concerned. That is indicative of poor customer service. If it starts out this way, I can only imagine how it will end. This is just a simple man’s opinion, hopefully it helps someone.
reviews.ownerResponse: Hello, Vince.  We're sorry we couldn't schedule your mom as soon as you'd like.  We are a very small and busy office.  If you'd like that list of other elder law firms we offered to see if you can find a sooner appointment, please call back and we'd be happy to send it to you.  You can also go to www.naela.org to search for another elder law attorney in her area.

reviews.text: Steve's attitude and treatment could have been better. I can see he would be a great trial lawyer, but don't treat your client like that. Fee's were 8K. I cancelled the case with Steve's firm to humble him. Had a great additional discovery in my case, handled it myself, and I made one phone call and my case with DHHS was overturned, rightfully so. That simple. I could be wrong but Steve Smith knew about a typo in my pervious submissions to DHHS before I even brought him a copy of my submissions? Granted it was an awkward typo, but how did he know about it?  My discoveries and findings would have been an excellent Lawsuit on DHHS and the Social workers making positive changes for families and children. I really thought Steve would be the one to go to. I am sure Steve will know who I am. The medical records auditor for FWA and patient quality improvement programs that's had success protecting families and children against DHHS in multiple cases and multiple states because I am right. I even thought at one small moment to offer my services free to his firm to consult on medical kidnapping cases due to being successful, but I will not be disrespected like that. Please learn from my submissions because I am right! I suspect they are also recording in their conference room so be aware. My employer is non of Steve Smith's business. If Steve Smith wishes to discuss this further he can contact me.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We strive to treat every client with professionalism, respect, and candor. We are sorry to hear that your experience did not meet your expectations. Clear communication and mutual understanding are important in any attorney-client relationship, and when a client chooses to move in a different direction, we respect that decision.

Because of our ethical obligations and the duty of confidentiality owed to all current and former clients, we cannot address or discuss any specific facts, strategy decisions, fee arrangements, or communications in a public forum. What we can say is that our firm approaches every matter thoughtfully, evaluates the information provided to us, and advises clients based on our professional judgment and the applicable law.

Regarding your concerns about privacy, we take client confidentiality seriously and operate in compliance with all professional and legal standards.

If you wish to discuss your situation or concerns further, please do not hesitate to contact us. We would be happy to hear your side where it can be discussed further.

reviews.text: I had a wonderful experience with Steve Smith and his team in helping me with my divorce process. It was lengthy, due to my divorcee living outside of the country, but Steve and his team were up to the challenge and guided me and supported me every step of the way. For cases like this, I wouldn't have chosen anyone else to help me. I am so grateful for Steve and his firm to have been an incredible support system for me through my divorce. Thank you so much for all your help!

reviews.text: Richard Lucas was wonderful to work with in my recent divorce case. He was extremely attentive to my divorce case, which was not a high value case for the firm, and also involved working with a difficult opposing party, and he was always available when I had important questions about how our approach or the opposing's would affect myself and my children. Most importantly, I could tell from day 1 that he truly cared about my well being and that of my 2 small children. He was extremely attentive to details and to timelines. Always professional, also very human and compassionate. Exactly what a single mom needs when getting divorced in a high-conflict but low value matter. Thank you Richard for your help! The paralegals at this firm are also wonderful! I would not recommend using Steve Smith himself as your lawyer (the reviews regarding him are negative and true), but Richard Lucas is a new and wonderful addition to his team. Thank you to Richard and the paralegal team for handling my case so well!

I do apologize for the experience you had with Steve, if you would like to discuss that further, please do not hesitate to contact us. Wishing you the best! :)

After carefully reviewing our records, we’re unable to find any consultation, matter, or phone interaction associated with the name provided in your review. That said, we genuinely want to understand what may have occurred and ensure that any concerns are addressed appropriately.

reviews.text: This law firm took my 300$ consultation fee, knowing that they would not be able to take my case. I was supposed to be scheduled with Steve, Somebody else stepped in, and her words were exactly I was looking before we had this meeting and there’s nothing that I can do. When I gave my money I was took they took cases like mine all the time, far from the truth. People like this is not what you want when you want to fight for something!
reviews.ownerResponse:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We’re sorry to hear about your frustration and understand how upsetting it can be when expectations aren’t met during an already difficult situation.

The purpose of a consultation is to review the details of your particular circumstances and determine, based on that information, whether our firm is the right fit to take the case. In some instances, after a thorough review, we may determine that we’re unable to move forward with representation. When that happens, it’s not a reflection of the importance of the matter, but rather our professional obligation to be honest and ethical about whether we can meaningfully assist.

We also understand your concerns regarding scheduling and communication and take feedback like this seriously. We would welcome the opportunity to review what occurred and ensure our consultation process and expectations were handled appropriately.

If you’d like to discuss this further, please contact our office directly and ask to speak with management. We appreciate you bringing this to our attention and wish you the best moving forward.

reviews.text: We had a terrible experience with Steve Smith and his firm. He is the type of attorney that gives attorneys a bad reputation. We had a straightforward case that was not handled well. Steve is unprofessional and did not take the time to stay up to date on our case or review notes before meetings. He insulted my fiance and I on multiple occasions with comments that were personal and offensive. The office staff is no better and there is clearly a culture at this firm. We could have done a better job representing ourselves without an attorney. I do not recommend them at all and would urge someone to look elsewhere.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We’re sorry to hear that this was your experience. At Steve Smith Trial Lawyers, we take concerns about professionalism, communication, and client care very seriously, and it’s never our intention for any client to feel disrespected or unheard.

Because confidentiality is central to the attorney–client relationship, we’re unable to address specific details in a public forum. That said, the feedback you’ve shared does not reflect the standards we hold ourselves to, and we would welcome the opportunity to better understand what occurred.

We encourage you to contact our firm directly so we can review your concerns and address them appropriately. Client trust and respect are priorities for us, and we are always open to constructive feedback that helps us improve.

reviews.text: Steve Smith and his team will hit the ground running and provide excellent legal services in a no nonsense and professional way.

Steve Smith and his team are not only extremely knowledgeable, but supportive and sensitive to the fact that a child custody case can be emotionally distressing. Steve and his team are highly responsive, check in frequently, make sure I understand what is happening at all times, and explain everything to me in terms I can easily understand.
If you're seeking legal help as a child custody client, call Steve Smith’s legal team today.

My ex husband used this lawyer after I left an abusive four year relationship to take my son from me. My ex husband told me he wanted primary custody- and that his lawyer “Stephen Smith” wanted me to sign my parental rights responsibilities away but he “wouldn’t let his lawyer do that”  (this is all in text conversation) at this point my son was being withheld from me for over six months, my attorney could not show up for our case, I had just gotten a new place and was spending money I didn’t have so I decided to sign an agreement with no representation at Stephen Smiths office (my ex husbands lawyer) I showed up, signed the paper and left. Only to find out I had indeed signed away my parental responsibilities and rights. I know stephen smith orchestrated this as my ex husband expressed in text that he did not agree with Stephen wanting to do that and would not let him trick me into signing it away. Two years later, my ex husband blocked me a year ago (last October after my son and I discussed trick or treating together)  I have not seen or talked to my son in a year. I do not know what my son thinks. I served my ex husband and did not file for a modification (6months ago)- I still have no lawyer at this point so I was holding off on filing. For having my ex husband served I owe Stephen (2,500$) for filing against me after I had my ex husband served. I hope Stephen uses some of this money to get his mental health in check, if not a face lift will surely help with the sagging and frown lines for his next court appearance. I am spending the next year saving up, I have documentation of everything. Once I can get representation and get in front of a judge this will come to a swift end, I will be able to see my son. I hope this law office will be held responsible for their part (they should be shut down). If any lawyers who read this want to reach out please do, I am starting to share my story and have all documentation.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We are truly sorry to hear how painful and distressing this situation has been for you. Matters involving children and family relationships are deeply emotional, and we recognize how difficult these experiences can be.

That said, our firm strongly disputes the allegations made in this review. Attorneys are ethically and legally prohibited from misleading any party, particularly in family law matters, and we do not “orchestrate” outcomes or advise individuals to waive parental rights without proper legal process and safeguards in place. Any agreements executed in our office are governed by court procedures and judicial approval, not unilateral action by counsel.

Because client confidentiality and court rules prevent us from discussing the details of any case in a public forum, we cannot address specific claims here. However, we take concerns about professionalism and ethics seriously. If you believe there has been an error or misconduct, we encourage you to raise those concerns through the appropriate legal channels so they can be reviewed fully and fairly.

We sincerely hope you are able to obtain the representation and support you need moving forward and wish you the best as you continue to pursue resolution through the court system.

reviews.text: Waste of money! My advice find someone else!
reviews.ownerResponse: We understand how frustrating legal outcomes can be. What someone hopes will happen and what the law allows are often very different, and results are ultimately determined by facts, evidence, and the court—not personal preference. Our role is to advocate aggressively within the bounds of the law and work toward the best possible outcome given those realities. We regret that your experience did not meet your expectations and wish you the best moving forward.

reviews.text:
A good rule of thumb: never trust a law office that claims to specialize in everything—medical malpractice, divorce, wills and estates, criminal law, and more. Real professionals focus and build expertise. When a firm spreads itself thin across so many areas, you’re not getting true specialists but rather generalists who claim to do it all, but rarely do anything well.

Steve Smith Trial Lawyers is a perfect example of this problem. From my experience, there is a constant turnover in both attorneys and support staff, which is a major red flag. A reputable firm retains skilled employees who bring stability and experience to clients’ cases. Instead, this office feels like a revolving door, which makes it nearly impossible to trust that anyone there will follow through or even stay long enough to see a case through properly.

If you happen to be unlucky enough to get assigned a Steve Smith attorney through the criminal justice system, do yourself a favor and request a different lawyer immediately. Having competent, stable representation is critical in those circumstances, and this firm does not provide it.

What you end up with is a ragtag group of unprofessionals, operating more like a patchwork crew than a serious law practice. Professionalism, consistency, and competence should be the bare minimum for a law firm, and unfortunately, Steve Smith Trial Lawyers doesn’t meet that standard.
If you value your time, money, and peace of mind, I strongly suggest looking elsewhere.
reviews.ownerResponse:
We appreciate you taking the time to share your perspective. While we respectfully disagree with the characterizations in this review, we recognize that clients and community members may have differing views on how law firms structure their practices.

Our firm is built around experienced attorneys who focus on specific practice areas within a collaborative team model, allowing clients to benefit from both subject-matter knowledge and collective resources. We are committed to professionalism, ethical representation, and continuity of care in every matter we handle.

Because we cannot discuss individual experiences or assignments publicly, we encourage anyone with concerns to reach out directly or seek independent counsel to ensure they feel confident in their representation.

reviews.text:
I had the great pleasure of working with Richard Lucas and Tyler Soucy. They were both professional, responsive, and, most importantly, very passionate about my case.

An elected official was attempting to interfere with my First Amendment rights, in what was an unprecedented case in this particular state.

Not only did Mr. Lucas and Mr. Soucy understand the facts of my case, but they also grasped the precedent it would set. In addition to fully reviewing the evidence and exhibits I provided, they went above and beyond to uncover other exhibits about the defendant that I did not have in my possession.

On the day of the trial, Mr. Soucy was masterful in his cross-examination; he slowly built our case to expose the defendant's frivolous attempt to use the court to chill free speech. I lost count of how many hypocritical corners he backed the defendant into.

To say that I highly recommend the team at Steve Smith Trial Lawyers would be an enormous understatement. Mr. Lucas and Mr. Soucy are both champions for justice and incredible trial attorneys. I was very fortunate to have worked with these fine gentlemen.

We paid $300 for an online consultation that he never showed up to. Every step of our adoption has been made impossible for one reason or another. This is just the newest road block.
*** Edit, after a few rescheduling errors on their part we finally connected. After a brief 20 minute meeting we are no closer and $300 further away from adoption. Everything they told us wasn't true. Like not even close.
Complete scam artists.
Waisted our money. Google did a better job.
reviews.ownerResponse: Thank you for taking the time to share your experience, and we want to sincerely apologize for the frustration and disappointment this situation has caused — especially during what we know is already a deeply emotional and complex journey. We did encounter unexpected issues with our scheduling platform, and unfortunately, your consultation was one of the appointments affected. This is not the level of service we hold ourselves to, and we take full responsibility. Our team acted quickly to address the issue and ensure all impacted appointments were rescheduled as a top priority. We understand how important every step of the adoption process is, and the last thing we want is to add more stress. Your journey is important, and we’re here to support you however we can. If you'd like to connect directly so we can assist further, please don’t hesitate to reach out to our team.

No suggestions ,no information on what I could do in which I already knew what I can do. Is just pay the bill, but if I'm paying you five grand for some advice you need to give me some advice. But I suggest you look around in the state of Maine if you want a good lawyer because this is not the place to go. One thing I will be in court in the State of Maine again .That's for dam sure.

This team takes their time for each and every case), Renee for her assertiveness and being astute, Tyler for helping when Steve was not available, and being assertive and helping me navigate the questions when we had to speak in front of a judge. This divorce was not easy for me, the most emotional time I have gone through, and I felt that without my lawyer in court the day I had seen my former spouse for the first time in months, I would have shut down completely, but I didn't.

prrofessionally respectful. And a highly respected Squire among his profession.. but a no-nonsense kind of a lawyer.

Bob did very little to aid me or answer questions. During most phone calls he was unsure as to which case he was talking about, multiple times making statements about my case that were not about my case at all.

I asked Bob for a meeting to discuss my case to evaluate if he was fit to represent me. Immediately after asking for a meeting he filed his withdrawal.

Not family oriented. Bob was the opposing attorney In my divorce. In response to my motion he called me selfish in court document s because I wanted to *gasp* see my own children. Bob concocted this scenario l that I could not see my own children because of the pandemic and seemingly advised my ex wife to not exchange the children if they were coming to their home in MA. Despiite having arrangement for covid testing at Emerson Hospital Urgent Care, Bobs actions resulted in my children not being able to sleep in their own beds here at their house in Massachusetts for approximately 35 days.if you are a dad, caveat emptor. I feel badly for Bob's own kids.

Update to Bob's response:  the key to the review is that it states I already had arrangements with Emerson Hospital Urgent Care for testing. They could quarantine or provide a negative test. Peter Rodway: “In my experience as a criminal defense lawyer, some of the best places to get drugs are sober houses. I advise most of my clients to avoid them if they really want to stay sober. I handled a case recently in which two sober house residents at a sober house in Portland, high on drugs, robbed the Cumberland Farms on Pine Street with guns. They committed the robbery and went back to the sober house after they were done.
It goes without saying that for the Town to allow this to continue is inappropriate and wrong, and is in complete disregard of the rights of the property owners in that area to enjoy our property. I really hope that rights of the property owners don’t wind up taking a back-seat to the perceived housing rights, or claimed disability rights, of the ex-convicts.”

I owe a lot to this law firm. They are amazing at what they do. Dylan is a top notch criminal defense attorney, but with that said if I didn’t mention Connor Todd I would be doing viewers of this review a disservice. Young guy that really gets to know the client he’s working with. I think my outcome would have been much different if he did not give me the time and attention he did. I wish I could recommend these guys to everyone that’s on the ropes looking to counter a better outcome in the court room. If you have money to spend or get lucky enough to get these guys appointed from the courts sleep well knowing you have the absolute best you can get. Thanks for everything you have done for me and good luck in the future.

I would highly recommend Attorney Conner Todd to anyone and everyone….Thank You So Much Conner For All Your Help!!!!!

A huge thank you Connor for the long hours and years of work!

Most importantly, Ms. McDaniel kept us very informed every step of the way and billed us very fairly. I will absolutely hire her in the future should any need arise.

Sarah McDaniel represented my family in a zoning dispute with a Cumberland County town and an abutting property owner. She was recommended by another attorney who had a conflict and was unable to take our case. I owe that attorney a big thank you! Right from the start Sarah was very professional yet friendly, putting us at ease with the process. She took our hundreds of pages of poorly organized research and made sense out of it, added to it, and came up with a course of action and grounds to file an appeal. Sarah never held out false hope and was very honest in her opinion for our chances to prevail. She was also upfront on what to expect in overall costs to proceed with the action. We were kept well informed by her during the entire process. Always responded to emails promptly and returned phone calls in a timely manner. All of our questions were always answered fully and any suggestions we had were well considered by her. Our appeal was successful and I can honestly give Sarah our highest recommendation. Our appeal was successful which we attribute to Sarah’s dedication and diligence. Definitely a ten out of ten.

I was involved in a motor vehicle accident in September of 2011. Things were moving along in a positive manner with the insurance carrier of the other driver (who was at fault.) Then things began to get complicated and I knew I was in over my head. I had no interest in calling the "self proclaimed" personal injury specialists on TV. If I was going to bring in an attorney, I wanted someone with integrity, respect, knowledge and professionalism. Tom was referred to me by a friend and I will say that after my first meeting with him, the cynicism and fears over bringing an attorney into this situation immediately went away. He brought all of those qualities to the table and I felt confident in his abilities as we moved forward. He and his staff worked in a very professional manner to keep me well informed during every step of process along the way. He kept me updated and discussed each point as it came up to ensure I was making informed decisions. It was a two year process to negotiate with the insurance but I was very pleased with the resolution of my case. I know that Tom and his staff worked their hardest to find the best resolution for me and my family. He was honest and forthright and did not make big promises or claims simply to get me to sign on with him. If you are looking for a personable, professional and most importantly knowledgable attorney to help you navigate uncertain waters - I know from personal experience that Tom Douglas is the attorney to help you. Just as he helped me and my family.

Mr. Douglas and his assistant did a good job representing me in my car accident case. He was always polite, professional, efficient, easy to talk to, answered my emails promptly and did a good job explaining all the details.  I am happy to recommend him to others.
I couldn’t recommend them enough. Will and Erica made a worlds difference for me and really put my mind at ease through the whole process. Very grateful to have found them!!

Will Ashe is a supremely competent defense attorney.  He’s articulate, intelligent, sincere, and passionate about his approach to his work.  If you’re looking to fight whatever it is you’ve been accused of, give him a call.  If anyone can win it, he can.

I highly recommend Attorney Will Ashe. I was recently charged with a low-level offense in Hancock County, and as a sitting legislator, my case came with added complexity beyond the legal issues — including public and press considerations. Will handled everything with exceptional professionalism, discretion, and care.
He took the time to talk me through each step, made sure I fully understood my options, and helped me make sound decisions. Will went above and beyond what most criminal defense attorneys would typically handle, managing both the legal defense and the broader strategy that my situation required.
Thanks to his thorough work and steady guidance, the case was dropped in about six weeks. I’m deeply grateful for his skill, judgment, and support throughout the process. If you’re looking for a capable, thoughtful, and trustworthy defense attorney, Will Ashe is outstanding

I had a great experience with this law firm. Erica was especially excellent- friendly, professional, and very timely from start to finish. Being listened to helped me feel completely understood. Thanks so much.

I hired Attorney Ashe for a DWI case in Bar Harbor maine while working for the summer. I was charged with a DWI & wreckless driving even though I was a .05 BAH and did not wreck but merely did not use a turn signal and I was speeding according to the Bar Harbor police, even though they never clcoked my speed. Attorney Ashe got a plea bargain to have the dWI dropped and after 1 year the wreckless driving would be dropped in favor of a simple ticket for imprudent speed. To make matters worse, I was charged for another DWI in Holden,ME shortly after. I once again was under the legal limit and I was pulled over for having a tag light out. My BAH was tested and it came out to 0.07 but I was once again charged with a DWI. I quickly called Attorney Ashe and explained my situation. Attorney Ashe was very empathetic and said I was more likely to.be struck by lightning than to be charged for.two under the limit DWI's. Attorney Ashe told me he would only charge me if the case was not dismissed and that he would.only charge half his fee if the case was tried. Attorney Ashe and his team quickly drew up a letter of representation to be sent to the DA's office and shortly after the case was quickly dismissed. Attorney Ashe is known around the courts as an aggressive defense attorney and is know to not negotiate deals for his clients if he feels they have a real shot at winning at trial. To call Attorney Ashe a pit bull is understatement, but calling him the best defense attorney in Maine would be the best title for him and his team. 15/10 stars for Attorney Ashe and his team. Attorney Ashe and his team saved me not once but twice from a lot of legal trouble that the Maine legal system wanted to throw the book at me for.  I didn't know Attorney Ashe wanted to be an Attorney since he was a child or that he was voted one of the best trial lawyers in the U.S before I hired him, but I did after doing some research and reading his legal bio online. Do yourself a favor and hire Attorney Ashe and his team as you won't find better.

Thank you Attorney Ashe & Erica.

Sincerely,
Justin

reviews.text: I worked with Alex Spadinger and Tara Lake for my motor vehicle accident. My case was a complicated case with multiple stakeholders and varying conflicts of interest. Highly recommend working with Alex and his team - he gets complicated work done!

reviews.text:
@ first working with a court appointed lawyer I was very skeptical. At this point I really had no choice but to put my trust in someone I did not know from a hole in the wall. I got pulled over for my head light out and a simple traffic stop turned to a nightmare really fast due to my past. I was on the side of the road for 2 hours with this police officer in my face telling me I was on something and the more he harassed me the more he was triggering my anxiety and ptsd. No more how many times I told this police officer I wasn’t on anything he still continues to ask me what are you on, I know you are on something. It didn’t stop there though he kept going with the harassment on telling me he knows I am on something and arrested me. I sat in the police cruiser for another 45 minutes watching him ram sack my car for drugs that definitely wasn’t there. This police officer had me so scared I just wanted to get to the police station and I soon learned I would be there for another two hours being harassed. My breathalyzer came back 0.00, I failed more test at the police station than I did on road side. So after all said and done they asked me for a blood test I definitely agree to that the arresting officers was shocked on that one came back with no Narcotic drugs in my system. My whole life was being destroyed by a Police officer that was taking his badge and using it for power.

Matt, Kept me out of the jail time I was facing, he kept me out of a year probation, and he kept me out of substance abuse counseling. Mean time in these 8 months of hell he made it so I would be able to drive 90 percent of this time. After said and done I hired him on to do my motor vehicle hearing. And at the end of the day this was a learning experience I never want to face again, but I did not lose my license. Matt checked in on me weekly to see how I was or highly professional on telling me my next steps on what I needed to do. Matt was highly professional with all the moves he was making in my defense and definitely a caring compassionate lawyer.
I don’t hug very many people but the day I won my motor vehicle hearing I had to give this man a hug. He saved my life from being destroyed by a power trip police officer. And for that I will forever be grateful. It goes 50/50 with this one he worked hard to help me and my honesty was the other big price in this, I see people say court appointed lawyers are not going to help, I see it differently they work with what you give them to work with.
Matt is very knowledgeable and professional. He kindly made sure I fully understood all my options and requirements.
reviews.ownerResponse: We are thrilled to hear from you, Lori! We love to see feedback from our clients. Thank you for the kind words.

After enduring a 6-year legal battle, I can confidently say that Attorney Trey Cox didn’t just resolve my case — he helped me finally close one of the most painful and stressful chapters of my life. My case began back in 2021 and dragged on for years due to a clear conflict of interest involving the original prosecuting attorney, Sarah Shannon. Despite being fully cooperative — on bail the entire time without a single issue, not even a traffic ticket — she continuously refused to re-offer a fair deal that should have been on the table from the beginning.

The emotional toll was overwhelming. I had gone through multiple attorneys before Attorney Trey Cox — most either didn’t take my case seriously or simply couldn’t make any progress. I was stuck in limbo for years, losing hope that I would ever see the end of it.

But from the moment I hired Attorney Cox, everything changed.

He was incredibly responsive, professional, and truly committed to helping me. He kept me updated at every stage, returned every call and message, and took the time to explain the legal process clearly — something no other attorney had done before. I never felt like “just another client.” He made me feel like my case — and my peace of mind — truly mattered to him.

Once the case was reassigned to a new prosecutor, Attorney Cox went to work. He handled everything with skill and determination, and after years of delays and disappointment, he was finally able to get the original plea offer reinstated. Because of him, my case was successfully resolved on September 19, 2025.

I am beyond grateful for Attorney Cox. He didn’t just get me a legal result — he gave me my life back. After six long years of stress, uncertainty, and feeling stuck, I can finally move forward. That feeling of relief and closure is something I can’t put into words, and I owe it entirely to his hard work, dedication, and belief in my case.

If you’re looking for a lawyer who is not only experienced and professional, but who will actually fight for you, stand by you, and see your case through to the end, I cannot recommend Attorney Cox highly enough. He changed my life, and I will always be thankful.

Attorney Trey Cox is a passionate, warm and wonderful lawyer who I would recommend to anyone. He lives and breathes his cases. He responds quickly to all contact and his ethical bent makes him a perfect choice for anyone seeking justice in their circumstances. I am so grateful to him and I am sure Thistle, Weaver & Morris saw what I saw too. Trey has a very bright future in his home state, doing what he loves and what he was born to do.

When I started out with Jackson and MacNichol I had a male lawyer who really cared about my case.  He is a veteran himself and he checked in with me regularly and if I called he took my calls, I never had to wait for him to call me back.  He replied promptly to my emails too.  He got me 50 percent for service connection (which was three months after the hearing) which honestly I was not expecting anything at that point because this has been an upward battle since 2000.  Then this lawyer left Jackson and MacNichol.  I asked him to take my other cases with him but he said he couldn't.  I ended up with a new lawyer (Tiffany Koustis) with lead attorney (Alexandra Jackson) This lawyer has done the bare minimum for my other cases.  Her written closing argument repeated the same points over and over again like a dead horse, she cited no case law for the judge to reference and left out names of VA doctors who stated in writing that my conditions are service connected.  She also left out dates/times of events.  After I read the closing argument I sent an email to the law firm.  It has now been 9 months since this last hearing and the judge has not come back with a ruling.   So now Ms. Tiffany Koustis decided to send the Judge yet a second closing argument 8 months after her disasterous first one.  The second closing argument cited case law but still didn't give dates/times and didn't include VA doctors who agree my conditions are service connected.  I'm sure the judge is thinking that this law firm doesn't take him seriously at this point and by now he has forgotten everything in the case files and what I said during the hearing.  Long story short I regret staying with Jackson and MacNichol.  I should have forced them to give my case to the Veteran lawyer that left them.  I want to make clear that I provided Jackson and MacNichol with letters from three doctors two are VA doctors one is a specialist from Tampa General who stated my conditions are service connected.  Lastly the judge in my last case ruled in my favor and went further in writing in her decision letter that these other conditions are also service connected.  Yet here we are 9 months after my hearing and have no decision.

I would like to sincerely thank Jackson & MacNichol Law Firm for their tireless efforts in helping me achieve a 100% VA disability rating. reviews.text: Worst experience ever all they did was file my claim I had a veteran nonprofit help me with everything and when I did call the staff was rude don't use this service ever they took 30 percent and did not work thank for nothing veterans beware

For over 9 years now I’ve been waiting for my inherent rights as a disabled veteran and Jackson&MacNichol have repeatedly told me to be patient about my 100% disability appeal! But all that’s happened is more waiting for something to happen, but the only thing that has happened is asking for new forms to be filled out, over and over and over again. As of September 27 2025 it’s been 9 years going nowhere and I’ve repeatedly asked the Attorneys to contact the office of The Inspector General to demand that the VA act on the Order Of Judge Doolittle that he remanded my case back to the Veterans Administration to correct their mistakes in my records which indicated that I am 100% disabled. That was over 3 years ago and still nothing has been done about this matter by my attorneys at Jackson&MacNichol, then over 3 years ago the VA granted my current wife as my dependent and indicated that payment would be processed, but nothing was processed and now a person I’ve never heard of at Jackson&MacNichol; Jennifer Contreras, reached out to my email wanting more details about previous marriage which I supplied over 3 years ago. In the last month I’ve sent more messages to my Attorney Devin Sorkin and Andrea Jackson without any response from either of them! Additionally I’ve sent messages to the Attorney representative Megan Potter for over two weeks now and no response either! What sort of Legal Representation is this supposed to represent for any disabled Veteran? I’m just supposed to continue being patient with repeatedly filling out new forms and getting them more information from doctors and hospitals and historical records? Who’s working for me, besides myself? I’ve actually begged for help when I was homeless and living in my car and no one did anything about it only filled out another form and afterwards they didn’t pursue anything else to help me. This continued neglect has led to such deep depression and emotional distress that I’ve attempted suicide several times in these 9 years of waiting for someone at Jackson&MacNichol to do something other than telling me to be patient? In those 9 years Jackson&MacNichol I have supplied the attorneys with over 1300 pages of documents and evidence and met with several professional doctors who have also provided evidence of my 100% disability! But still nothing has been done by the attorneys at Jackson&MacNichol! All I see is Nothing being done and only promises and promises and promises and asked to be patient? Where does patience run out and neglect come in by the attorneys who are supposed to be responsible and representing me as a Disabled Marine Corps Veteran since 1972 over 53 years ago! I’ve lost all hope and faith in my attorneys and now I’m just waiting to die and preparing for my cemetery plot and funeral plans. Since I’ve had 2 heart attacks and an Aortic Valve replacement and several minor strokes as well as multiple seizures from my injuries in the Marine Corps, with several serious seizures being near death seizures! But who cares about it or my life and my family at Jackson&MacNichol? NO ONE!Additionally, Devin Sorkin and Andrea Jackson were negligent with my documents medical records that I’d sent them over the past 9 years. I’d requested them to file my Cervical Spinal Stenosis associated The diagnosis of spinal stenosis and Epileptic seizures with my Cervical Spine injuries in the Marine Corps in 1972. I submitted documentation and medical records for over 6 years concerning my Diagnosis from medical Neurologist and treatments at multiple Veterans Medical Centers and hospitals which they failed to provide and failed to do. Over 9 years of waiting for them to do as they were supposed to as legal representation for my veterans disability and they did nothing. And recently Devin Sorkin just dumped my case without saying anything to me or advising me who’s supposed to be representing me. There are a few good attorneys at Jackson&MacNichol, but Devin Sorkin and Andrea Jackson are not included as good attorneys for their failure in professional representation .

I am absolutely thrilled with the outcome of the work by Jackson and MacNichol. Without them I know the results would have not been possible. I would strongly recommend to all my Veteran colleagues. Thanks again

reviews.ownerResponse:
OK, here's the story: In 2014, Robert was charged with felony drunk driving since he had 2 prior convictions for OUI. He then violated bail by drinking and was charged with that crime too. With another lawyer, he worked out a deal to drop the felony OUI down to a misdemeanor if he complied with conditions for 2 years. He needed to obey the law, get treatment, pay some fees and return to court with proof to have the charges reduced. Robert moved to Florida, failed to comply with conditions and never came back to court for his scheduled return date in 2016.

A warrant of arrest was issued. That warrant was active for years until Robert hired me in November of 2020 to try to undo the mess that he had created. We successfully got the warrant recalled and we rescheduled the case for further proceedings. The district attorney was not impressed that Robert had been at large for over six years and was not inclined to do anything to get the felony charges dropped. Still, negotiations continued. Eventually, the prosecutor agreed to give Robert the good outcome negotiated back in 2014 as long as he continued to obey the law and did 60 hours of community service work. Robert was expected to have this work completed by October 2021.

Unfortunately, Robert did not complete the community service work and in August 2021 was arrested in Florida and sentenced to serve nine months on assault charges. Because of this, he lost the chance for the misdemeanor drop-down and the prosecutor insisted that his felony OUI plea must stand. Under the terms of the 2014 deal, Robert was exposed to five years in prison if he violated. Despite this, I was able to negotiate an agreement where Robert would be sentenced only to time already served in jail, the minimum OUI fine and the minimum 6 year license suspension.

So I guess that is a long way of saying that it was not any failure of mine that caused this longer suspension. Robert had every opportunity to help himself and resolve this case in 2016 with a 3 year suspension. When he failed to comply with that deal and a warrant went out, he hired me. Somehow, I was able to get him the same good deal as long as he committed no crimes and did some volunteer work. When he failed to do that, any ability to get the felony dropped went out the window and Robert was exposed to up to 5 years in prison. Even so, I was able to get him time served and the minimum suspension allowed. Is 6 years of suspension too long? Sure it is, but the only person who had control over that was Robert. If he had complied with the deal, he would have cut the suspension in half. I can understand his frustration with this outcome, it’s a long suspension. Rather than take responsibility for his own actions, Robert now chooses to blame me.

I recently was visiting the area and paid to park across from their firm, unbeknownst to me, they “own” the spot I paid for until 5 o’clock… The moment my family and I exited the car, they had someone calling to have my car towed right away. Snowballing the entire day into quite the negative ordeal. Yes, I realize this has nothing to do with their legal representation, BUT, their complete lack of compassion, empathy or care was very clear. I had dealings with two individuals specifically but, Nancy  especially didn’t care in the least about my HONEST and paid for mistake. Her downright rude and cold demeanor should have turned me off right away but she was the one I was told to deal with. The first time I asked to speak with her she was just plain mean and it was clear she has zero ability to listen to concerns and be compassionate. The second time I needed to speak with her, she refused to come speak with me and sent out one of the firms partners to handle my issue (handle being a very loose term)  It boils down to this: parking lot drama is something that happens often. The parking company who owns and manages the lot and the tow company were very fed up with having to deal with this same story over and over again- even naming the same employee before I even had a chance to. This law firm benefits from calling and having cars towed and enjoys the entertainment factor of watching through their window while families or people are in distress because they followed all signs and prompts and likely PAID for a spot. The firm has 3 signs placed in this lot, leading patrons to believe the other spots are available for use if paid via the app listed on the sign. This is a known issue, the firm has been advised they can simply pay for 3 more signs and avoid this saga all together (again, since it is  a common happening) but they choose to just lean on the vague signage and lack of compassion to, what I can only assume, gain some type of monetary or comedic kickback. I can’t speak to their ability to practice law, but if they treat their clients like they do patrons off the street, I’d avoid thier representation at all costs.

reviews.text:
A short time ago, I was in motor vehicle accident. Being rear-ended while driving home. I was taken to the hospital, received treatment and was bed ridden for months..My vehicle was totalled.
I needed to seek help, due to the insurance companies were only wanting to pay minimal amounts in my opinion. A friend of mine who is an business attorney suggested Rob Stephen,due to his well known experience in personal injury claims.
I called his office and was pleasantly greeted Amy who took down all relevant info. Rob Stephen shortly called me back and said he would immediately start work on my case. Due to the fact that I was bed-ridden, Rob came to my house to gather more info and personally explain all possible avenues with going forward, what to expect, times involved, all medical bills (present and future) and various future outcomes in my case based on his vast experience.
Rob and Amy put my mind to rest, Rob dealt with all billing issues and negotiations with medical debt (insurances). He and Amy actually put my wife's worries to rest also..(happy wife is a happy life).
Soon, as predicted by Rob and Amy, the time frames were all met, and benchmarks on my case whizzed bye.
The whole time, I was kept completely up to date on all correspondence between all parties involved..Rob diplomatically and professionally negotiated through much difficulty an amount that exceeded all of our highest expectations.

reviews.text: Great firm, excellent lawyers.

I cannot express enough gratitude to Mike, Cat, and their entire team for the exceptional work they did on my visa case. From start to finish, their professionalism, clarity, and efficiency truly stood out.

Even though I worked with them remotely while living out of state, they made the entire process smooth and stress-free. From my very first consultation with Mike, I was impressed by how clear, thorough, and honest he was about the process and timeline. And they didn’t just meet expectations — they exceeded them, delivering results faster than anticipated and filing everything with precision and care.

Cat was consistently responsive, kept me updated at every stage, and took the time to explain both current steps and what to expect next. Their communication was always timely, kind, and reassuring.

If you’re looking for a team that is both professional and humane, I cannot recommend them highly enough. Thank you for your life-changing expertise and support — I’ll always be grateful.

Reliability, professionalism, kindness, promptness, perfectionism, outstanding customer service, this is how I would describe Mrs. Aga Asbury's traits, the attorney from Ford Murray company.

From the very beginning till the end of the process of getting my E-2 visa, every step of the way, Mrs. Asbury was there, she was patient, kind, humble, very supportive and did an excellent job, by doing her very best to make sure that we only succeed.

I love working with professionals only, because time is precious, and for such matters which are very important in life and business, You must be very precise in everything that You do.

I would highly recommend Mrs. Asbury and her team to anyone in this world that need a successful attorney for making sure that they get their flight ticket to USA.

Thank You very much for everything that You did for me, dear Aga!

May Lord God bless Ford Murray team and company!

Humbly Yours,

My husband and I had the pleasure of working with Aga Asbury and her legal team for our marriage-based green card application, and we could not be more pleased with the experience. From start to finish, their professionalism, expertise, and attentiveness made what could have been a stressful process incredibly smooth and straightforward.

I am incredibly grateful for their support and would highly recommend Aga's/FordMurray's services to anyone.

Aga Asbury and her team handled my L-1A New Office visa petition for a CEO and later Permanent Residency (Green Card) applications for me and my family. When I opened an office in California, I was searching for an experienced immigration lawyer who would help me get settled in the States with the right type of visa. From the start, Aga was very professional and helpful in navigating the intricacies of immigration law and procedures. She asked a lot of questions to gather the necessary information, explained which visa option was best for my situation and what could be expected during the process, and handled everything in a way that made the whole process as easy as possible. I was so impressed with Aga’s work that I asked for her help again when it was time to file for Permanent Residency for me and my family.

I highly recommend Aga to anyone who is looking for a great immigration attorney who truly cares about the outcome of their case. Her customer service, dedication, expertise, and commitment to client satisfaction are unparalleled. I am so grateful for her assistance and of course will not hesitate to seek her services again in the future.
I had an amazing experience with Aga Asbury and Ellen Shaw. Highly recommended service.

My husband and I worked with Aga and Ellen on an Adjustment of Status from a VWP to family based permanent resident status. I had a slight complication in that I required a vaccine waiver, which can be difficult to obtain.

Aga and Ellen helped us through the whole process with respect, efficiency and precision; the only request for evidence we received was related to the waiver. All our paperwork was perfect. Our whole case was completed within 6 months!

If you need help with your application I would recommend reaching out for the free consultation with Aga at the very least; speaking with her filled us with confidence to proceed, and we had spoken to a few other lawyers who actually had the opposite effect.

We will definitely reach out to Ford Murray again once we need to apply for citizenship as we can trust them to do all they can to help. They went above and beyond. Thanks Aga, Ellen and Ford Murray!

We are very thankful that Atty. Aga and staff helped us through the process of getting a K-1 visa. It was a success! I am here now in the USA with my wife! From all the law firm I emailed and contacted, she is the only one who replied with detailed answers to all my questions and I guarantee that she is always up to date regarding the news. Quick to response and also approachable. I couldn't ask for more! They are LGBTQ friendly! I am very lucky to have found her website and that I will recommend her law firm to others that need help! Thank you very much, Atty. Aga and Ellen and for all the staff members!

Aga was my lawyer who helped me get my green card. She made the process very easy and smooth for me. She had the knowledge on what and when to get things done and currently I have completed my process without any hiccups. She was also a great communicator and made sure I was aware of any changes or updates!

From the moment we called Aga and the staff at FordMurray, she put us at ease and we were much more comfortable with the immigration process. We were dealing with someone previously that was not half as knowledgeable. They walked us through each step and made sure we were prepared for all scenarios. Now I have my beautiful wife and daughters here as permanent residents on track to citizenship and all is good! We can't recommend FordMurray enough. A+ service from start to finish..
Aga is my lawyer. I used her services from a very first step to became a US citizenship. Her service is very professional. In the next future, my family and I are going to use her service again.

I hired Aga Asbury and her Ford Murray legal team to handle multiple E-2 visa petitions (first the initial petition and later the extensions) for me and my family. When I purchased a US-based business I needed to find an immigration attorney who would help me understand the best immigration options for my situation. From the very beginning, Aga was very professional and helpful in navigating the system. She conducted a very thorough interview to gather the necessary information, explained why E-2 visa option was best suited for my case, walked me through the application process and what I could expect, gave a detailed list of the documents and information that I needed to gather, and handled everything in a way that made the process understandable, as easy as possible, and in a manner that showed she truly cared about attaining the positive outcome I sought. Aga guided me through every step of the process with clarity and patience, making what could have been a daunting experience remarkably smooth and manageable. I was so pleased with the way Aga handled my E-2 petition, that of course I asked for her help with filing for E-2 extensions for me and my family.

I can't thank Aga and her team enough for their outstanding assistance with my E-2 petitions. From start to finish, Aga demonstrated unparalleled professionalism, expertise, and dedication. Working with Aga and her legal team was an absolute pleasure.

I highly recommend Aga and FordMurray to anyone in need of immigration services. Their professionalism, expertise, and commitment to client satisfaction are unmatched. I am grateful for their assistance and would not hesitate to seek their help again in the future.

Mike and his team are amazing! Right from the start with the initial call briefing on the whole process to US permanent residency, to the guidance with the excessive paperwork involved, being so responsive and patient with any doubts and multiple travel related questions - I was guided every step of the way. Mike made the whole process of obtaining the green card seamless and I am so grateful and appreciative! Would highly recommend Mike  and his team for any immigration related questions or issues!

I highly recommend Aga Asbury's services. We hired her to obtain a Green Card and the process with her was easy and smooth. From day one, Aga provided us with the information needed and she and her team accompanied us in the process. She gave us recommendations and tips about the best way to provide the needed information for the application. She answered questions and doubts and we felt extremely well supported during the process. As a result, I got the approval faster than expected. Therefore, I only have good words for the professionalism and excellent work that Aga did in this process, and I strongly recommend her if anyone is searching legal support in a similar process.
Great team. Highly recommend their services.

I am very thankful for Attorney Aga Asbury and the amazing support she gave me during my  green card application. Working with Aga and her team was fantastic and easy. They were professional and dedicated. Aga was very thorough, incredibly knowledgeable and extremely patient with all of my questions and concerns.
I would highly recommend Aga to anyone looking for immigration help. She showed true professionalism, expertise and dedication making sure I was happy. I am incredibly grateful for all the help she and her team gave me and would gladly recommend them to family and friends.

After everything, including appeals, we finally won; they found in our favor, so we don’t have to pay back anything! Throughout the entire process, Bain Testa was compassionate, patient, and always willing to explain what was happening and what to expect next.
